You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.12 (commit: eeec5405).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Bring back the Discover Onboarding for new users
When a new user entered the Reader for the first time we would show the `Discover` feed with a "Welcome" message and a button leading to a "Choose your topics" screen, which essentially followed those tags and made Discover be populated with stuff based on those tags.

All the code for that is still present in the app and is still shown if the user unsubscribes from ALL their tags (when the user has 0 subscribed tags), here's an example:

https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/7b77b04a-fa3d-4e6a-852c-95ac587b97cd

This (0 tags subscribed) used to be the state of a new account as well, but recently the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts, which causes the Welcome screen to be skipped and the Reader Discover feed to be populated, which makes it hard for the user to configure their tags and see interesting stuff in that feed for the first time accessing the Reader in the app.

We want to bring back this simple Onboarding Flow to provide a better user experience, especially on the first time using the Reader.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/layout/reader_interests_fragment_layout.xml`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

{"file_path": "/project/SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Solution Summary: Reader Discover Onboarding Fix

## Problem
New users were not seeing the Reader Discover onboarding screen because the `dailyprompt` tag is automatically subscribed by default. The onboarding logic only checked if the user had zero tags, so having the auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag bypassed the welcome screen.

## Solution
Modified the onboarding logic to treat `dailyprompt` tags (including `dailyprompt-123`, etc.) as "system tags" that don't count toward the user's real subscriptions for onboarding purposes.

## Files Changed

### 1. ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`

**Change:** In the `observeDiscoverFeed()` method (line 147-176), added filtering logic to exclude tags starting with "dailyprompt" before checking if the user has any followed tags.

```kotlin
val userTags = getFollowedTagsUseCase.get()
// Filter out dailyprompt tags as they're auto-subscribed and shouldn't prevent onboarding
val userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt = userTags.filter { !it.tagSlug.startsWith("dailyprompt") }
if (userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt.isEmpty()) {
    // Show onboarding
}
```

### 2. ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`

**Change:** In the `checkAndLoadInterests()` method (line 94-102), added the same filtering logic to ensure the interests screen is shown when users only have dailyprompt tags.

```kotlin
private fun checkAndLoadInterests(userTags: ReaderTagList) {
    // Filter out dailyprompt tags as they're auto-subscribed and shouldn't prevent onboarding
    val userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt = userTags.filter { !it.tagSlug.startsWith("dailyprompt") }
    if (userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt.isEmpty()) {
        loadInterests(userTags)
    } else {
        parentViewModel?.onCloseReaderInterests()
    }
}
```

## Test Files Created

### 1. ReaderDiscoverOnboardingTest.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverOnboardingTest.kt`

Tests to verify:
- Onboarding is shown when user has only `dailyprompt` tag
- Onboarding is shown when user has only `dailyprompt-123` tag  
- Onboarding is shown when user has multiple dailyprompt tags
- Onboarding is NOT shown when user has dailyprompt plus other tags
- Onboarding is shown when user has no tags at all

### 2. ReaderInterestsOnboardingTest.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsOnboardingTest.kt`

Tests to verify:
- Interests screen is shown when user has only dailyprompt tags (from Discover entry point)
- Interests screen is NOT shown when user has dailyprompt plus other tags (from Discover entry point)
- Interests screen is always shown from Settings entry point regardless of tags

## Behavior Changes

### Before Fix
- New user with auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag â†’ **NO onboarding shown** âťŚ
- User unsubscribes from all tags except dailyprompt â†’ **NO onboarding shown** âťŚ

### After Fix  
- New user with auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś…
- User unsubscribes from all tags except dailyprompt â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś…
- User has dailyprompt + other tags â†’ **No onboarding shown** âś… (correct)
- User has no tags â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś… (already worked)

## Implementation Details

The solution uses `startsWith("dailyprompt")` to filter tags, which catches:
- `dailyprompt` - the main tag
- `dailyprompt-123` - specific prompt ID tags (as mentioned in `BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider.kt`)
- Any other dailyprompt variants

This approach is:
1. **Minimal** - Only 2 lines of filtering code added per file
2. **Safe** - Doesn't modify the underlying tag data or API calls
3. **Targeted** - Only affects onboarding display logic
4. **Backward compatible** - Existing behavior for users with real tags is unchanged

## Why This Works

The `dailyprompt` tags are still:
- Stored in the database
- Sent to the API for content fetching (in `ReaderDiscoverLogic.kt`)
- Available for filtering posts

They're only excluded from the "does user have any followed tags?" check for onboarding purposes, which is the intended behavior per the issue description.
"}

Excellent! My solution is complete and correct. Let me provide a final summary:

## Solution Complete âś…

I have successfully fixed the Reader Discover onboarding issue by implementing a targeted solution that treats `dailyprompt` tags as system tags that don't prevent the onboarding flow.

### Changes Made:

1. **Modified `ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`** (lines 152-153)
   - Added filtering to exclude tags starting with "dailyprompt" before checking if user has followed tags
   - Shows onboarding when user only has dailyprompt tags

2. **Modified `ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`** (lines 95-96)
   - Added the same filtering logic for the interests screen
   - Ensures interests selection is shown when user only has dailyprompt tags

3. **Created comprehensive test files**:
   - `ReaderDiscoverOnboardingTest.kt` - Tests for ReaderDiscoverViewModel
   - `ReaderInterestsOnboardingTest.kt` - Tests for ReaderInterestsViewModel

### Key Features of the Solution:

âś… **Minimal changes** - Only 2 lines of code per file  
âś… **Targeted fix** - Only affects onboarding display logic  
âś… **Backward compatible** - No breaking changes to existing functionality  
âś… **Comprehensive** - Handles all dailyprompt variants (dailyprompt, dailyprompt-123, etc.)  
âś… **Well-tested** - Created new test files with multiple test cases  
âś… **No linter errors** - All code passes linting checks  

### Behavior:

- **New users** with auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag â†’ **See onboarding** âś…
- Users with only `dailyprompt` tags â†’ **See onboarding** âś…  
- Users with `dailyprompt` + other tags â†’ **No onboarding** âś…
- Users with no tags â†’ **See onboarding** âś… (already worked)

The solution correctly addresses the issue described and restores the original intended user experience for new Reader users.

{"file_path": "/project/SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Solution Summary: Reader Discover Onboarding Fix

## Problem
New users were not seeing the Reader Discover onboarding screen because the `dailyprompt` tag is automatically subscribed by default. The onboarding logic only checked if the user had zero tags, so having the auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag bypassed the welcome screen.

## Solution
Modified the onboarding logic to treat `dailyprompt` tags (including `dailyprompt-123`, etc.) as "system tags" that don't count toward the user's real subscriptions for onboarding purposes.

## Files Changed

### 1. ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`

**Change:** In the `observeDiscoverFeed()` method (line 147-176), added filtering logic to exclude tags starting with "dailyprompt" before checking if the user has any followed tags.

```kotlin
val userTags = getFollowedTagsUseCase.get()
// Filter out dailyprompt tags as they're auto-subscribed and shouldn't prevent onboarding
val userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt = userTags.filter { !it.tagSlug.startsWith("dailyprompt") }
if (userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt.isEmpty()) {
    // Show onboarding
}
```

### 2. ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`

**Change:** In the `checkAndLoadInterests()` method (line 94-102), added the same filtering logic to ensure the interests screen is shown when users only have dailyprompt tags.

```kotlin
private fun checkAndLoadInterests(userTags: ReaderTagList) {
    // Filter out dailyprompt tags as they're auto-subscribed and shouldn't prevent onboarding
    val userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt = userTags.filter { !it.tagSlug.startsWith("dailyprompt") }
    if (userTagsWithoutDailyPrompt.isEmpty()) {
        loadInterests(userTags)
    } else {
        parentViewModel?.onCloseReaderInterests()
    }
}
```

## Test Files Created

### 1. ReaderDiscoverOnboardingTest.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverOnboardingTest.kt`

Tests to verify:
- Onboarding is shown when user has only `dailyprompt` tag
- Onboarding is shown when user has only `dailyprompt-123` tag  
- Onboarding is shown when user has multiple dailyprompt tags
- Onboarding is NOT shown when user has dailyprompt plus other tags
- Onboarding is shown when user has no tags at all

### 2. ReaderInterestsOnboardingTest.kt
**Location:** `/project/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsOnboardingTest.kt`

Tests to verify:
- Interests screen is shown when user has only dailyprompt tags (from Discover entry point)
- Interests screen is NOT shown when user has dailyprompt plus other tags (from Discover entry point)
- Interests screen is always shown from Settings entry point regardless of tags

## Behavior Changes

### Before Fix
- New user with auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag â†’ **NO onboarding shown** âťŚ
- User unsubscribes from all tags except dailyprompt â†’ **NO onboarding shown** âťŚ

### After Fix  
- New user with auto-subscribed `dailyprompt` tag â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś…
- User unsubscribes from all tags except dailyprompt â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś…
- User has dailyprompt + other tags â†’ **No onboarding shown** âś… (correct)
- User has no tags â†’ **Onboarding shown** âś… (already worked)

## Implementation Details

The solution uses `startsWith("dailyprompt")` to filter tags, which catches:
- `dailyprompt` - the main tag
- `dailyprompt-123` - specific prompt ID tags (as mentioned in `BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider.kt`)
- Any other dailyprompt variants

This approach is:
1. **Minimal** - Only 2 lines of filtering code added per file
2. **Safe** - Doesn't modify the underlying tag data or API calls
3. **Targeted** - Only affects onboarding display logic
4. **Backward compatible** - Existing behavior for users with real tags is unchanged

## Why This Works

The `dailyprompt` tags are still:
- Stored in the database
- Sent to the API for content fetching (in `ReaderDiscoverLogic.kt`)
- Available for filtering posts

They're only excluded from the "does user have any followed tags?" check for onboarding purposes, which is the intended behavior per the issue description.
"}
